author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
Findings from codebase exploration:
1. View infrastructure already exists: TensorInner has offset + strides + Arc<TensorStorage> for shared ownership
2. view_reshape() and squeeze/unsqueeze already do zero-copy on CPU
3. permute() ALWAYS copies data element-by-element — even on CPU where stride swap would work
4. GPU squeeze/unsqueeze do a wasteful CPU round-trip for what should be metadata-only ops
5. No slice/narrow operations exist yet

Plan:
A. Rewrite permute to be zero-copy via stride permutation (CPU + GPU)
B. Fix GPU squeeze/unsqueeze/flatten to avoid CPU round-trip (metadata-only)  
C. Add narrow() as a zero-copy view using offset adjustment
D. Add a contiguous() helper that materializes non-contiguous tensors on demand
